originalTitle: Dad facing difficult summer as food donations plunge
summary: Food banks and community larders are warning of an uncertain future.
content:
Matthew said he does not know what he would do without the community larder

A single father using a community larder to feed his family says he is facing a difficult summer as food donations fall and voucher schemes end.

Matthew uses the Millbrook Hub community larder in Castle Cary up to three times a week.

The father-of-two said: "Without it, I don't know what I'd do. The cost of living definitely has had an impact. Half the time, I can't afford food for myself."

Charity leaders have said donations to food banks and community larders have hit record lows and they face an uncertain future.

Matthew said this summer was also going to be very difficult due to Somerset Council ending a scheme where families of children on free school meals automatically get supermarket vouchers over holidays .

"I'm going to have to stretch my budget even more this month, with great difficulty," he added.

Former Victorian baths illuminated for first event

Charity welcomes Burnham pledge to end rough sleeping

Matthew, who cannot work at the moment because he is a full-time father for young children, said over the next six weeks he might have to call on friends to help him pay for food for his children.

However, council chiefs said food vouchers are still available through the Crisis and Resilience Fund, which replaced the previous Household Support Fund in April.

Due to food choice being limited at the larder, Matthew said he mainly used it for nappies, toilet roll, shampoo and some food for himself.

He then uses all the money he gets to pay for his children's food.

Recently, he has seen the number of donations at the larder fall and said everyone is "struggling" due to the cost of living crisis.

Mark Hutton said he has seen a "lack of donations and contributions in recent months"

Mark Hutton, who helps run the Millbrook Hub community larder, said he had seen first-hand the drop in donations.

"It's a combination of everything," he said, explaining that supermarkets had reduced the availability of some items while people were "trying to save money".

Foodbank charity Trussell Trust said its North Bristol and South Gloucestershire branch was now buying more of its own stock than ever - a record of one in every four of the 15,045 it gave out in 2025.

Ellen Wood, the branch's CEO, said everyone was "feeling the pinch", and had seen people struggling "year after year" despite "so much generosity" from the community.

Most of the food shared by Trussell Trust is given by the public using donation points in supermarkets, but Wood said the number of items coming through this route had also dropped.

Wood said that the charity cannot keep buying food as donations drop

"If this trend keeps happening one or two years down the line, I don't know," she said.

"We can't keep buying food [and] if people can't afford to donate the food then we'll struggle."

Loki Stokes, the food bank's food logistics manager, added that he has "never seen our stock this low".

"We try to hold a week's buffer across all nine outlets, but this summer that's getting hard to promise," he said.

A Somerset Council spokesperson said the authority recognised that many households continue to face financial pressures due to the ongoing cost of living crisis.

"These pressures will increase for many families over the summer holidays.

"The three years of government funding provided through the Crisis and Resilience Fund enable us to build on our strong partnerships with organisations across Somerset, helping to ensure that residents who need support can access it close to where they live," they added.

chineseTitle: 食物捐赠锐减，爸爸面临艰难的夏天
chineseSummary: 食品银行和社区食品储藏室警告未来的不确定性。
chineseContent:
马修说他不知道如果没有社区食品储藏室他会做什么

一位使用社区食品储藏室养家糊口的单亲父亲表示，随着食品捐赠的减少和代金券计划的结束，他正面临着一个艰难的夏天。

马修每周最多使用卡里城堡米尔布鲁克中心社区食品储藏室 3 次。

这位两个孩子的父亲说：“如果没有它，我不知道我会做什么。生活成本肯定产生了影响。有一半的时间，我自己买不起食物。”

慈善机构领导人表示，对食品银行和社区食品储藏室的捐款已创历史新低，他们面临着不确定的未来。

马修说，今年夏天也将非常困难，因为萨默塞特议会终止了一项计划，该计划让享受免费校餐的儿童家庭在假期自动获得超市优惠券。

他补充说：“这个月我将不得不进一步扩大预算，这非常困难。”

前维多利亚浴室为首场活动点亮灯光

慈善机构欢迎伯纳姆承诺结束粗暴睡眠

马修目前无法工作，因为他是幼儿的全职父亲，他说在接下来的六周内，他可能不得不打电话给朋友来帮助他支付孩子们的食物费用。

不过，市议会负责人表示，食品券仍然可以通过危机与复原基金获得，该基金于四月份取代了之前的家庭支持基金。

由于食品储藏室的食物选择有限，马修说他主要用它来买尿布、卫生纸、洗发水和一些自己吃的食物。

然后他用所有的钱来支付孩子们的食物。

最近，他看到食品储藏室的捐款数量下降，并表示由于生活成本危机，每个人都在“挣扎”。

马克·赫顿表示，他发现“近几个月来缺乏捐款和捐款”

帮助管理米尔布鲁克中心社区食品储藏室的马克·赫顿 (Mark Hutton) 表示，他亲眼目睹了捐款的下降。

“这是所有因素的结合，”他说，并解释说，超市减少了某些商品的供应，而人们“试图省钱”。

Foodbank 慈善机构 Trussel Trust 表示，其北布里斯托尔和南格洛斯特郡分行现在购买的自有股票比以往任何时候都多，创下了 2025 年发放的 15,045 股中四分之一的记录。

该分会的首席执行官埃伦·伍德(Ellen Wood)表示，每个人都“感受到了压力”，尽管社区给予了“如此慷慨”的帮助，但人们却“年复一年”地在苦苦挣扎。

特鲁塞尔信托基金会分享的大部分食物都是公众通过超市的捐赠点捐赠的，但伍德表示，通过这条路线运送的物品数量也有所下降。

伍德表示，随着捐款减少，慈善机构无法继续购买食物

“我不知道这种趋势是否会持续一两年，”她说。

“我们不能继续购买食物，如果人们无力捐赠食物，那么我们就会陷入困境。”

食品银行的食品物流经理洛基·斯托克斯补充说，他“从未见过我们的库存如此之低”。

“我们试图在所有九家门店保留一周的缓冲期，但今年夏天这很难承诺，”他说。

萨默塞特议会发言人表示，当局认识到，由于持续的生活成本危机，许多家庭继续面临财务压力。

“暑假期间，许多家庭的压力将会增加。

他们补充说：“通过危机与恢复基金提供的三年政府资金使我们能够与萨默塞特各地的组织建立牢固的合作伙伴关系，帮助确保需要支持的居民能够在他们居住的地方附近获得支持。”
